pr.title: Match Melee's version of __start.c to fix splitting issue
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Added a matched C/inline-asm implementation of Melee's startup code under src/dolphin/os/init, removed the old __start body from asm/init.s, adjusted init object ordering, renamed several early runtime symbols to SDK-like names, and simplified/fixed elf2dol section/BSS handling. The PR title says this was to fix a splitting issue; the diff shows the fix centered on making __start compile as its own object and making DOL section sizes/BSS calculation derive from program headers instead of Makefile-supplied sdata/sbss indices.
what_changed: Created src/dolphin/os/init/__start.c with __check_pad3, weak inline-asm __start, __init_registers, __init_data, __init_hardware, and __flush_cache in the .init section.
Created src/dolphin/os/init/__start.h with startup constants, fixed-address Pad3Button declaration, externs for OS/DB/runtime routines, and __rom_copy_info/__bss_init_info structs.
Removed the __start and related startup routines from asm/init.s while keeping early .init routines such as memcpy and TRK_memset there.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/os/init/__start.o to INIT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk after asm/init.o.
Extended Makefile includes with src/dolphin/os/init/ and changed the elf2dol invocation from passing sdata/sbss program-header indices and target platform to only passing input ELF and output DOL.
Renamed func_800031F4 to memcpy and func_80003268 to TRK_memset in asm/init.s and propagated callsite names across many asm files.
Renamed func_8034844C to OSResetSystem and updated callsites.
Adjusted asm data padding/layout fallout, including deleted extra NULL/.4byte entries in abort_exit.s, __ppc_eabi_init.s, extab.s, extabindex.s, and changed a bss skip size.

Reworked tools/elf2dol.c: removed special sdata/sbss size tracking and platform argument, fixed swaf16 typo to swap16, made add_bss compute BSS as first-BSS-start through last-BSS-end, and wrote DOL headers with aligned text/data section sizes plus zero padding.
smart_moves: Moved __start into C with targeted inline assembly instead of leaving the entire startup sequence in raw asm, which makes the startup flow more readable while preserving exact register/bootstrap behavior.
Kept critical low-level routines explicitly in the .init section via pragmas and __declspec(section ".init"), reducing the risk that the compiler/linker place startup helpers into normal text.
Matched SDK/runtime symbol names such as memcpy, TRK_memset, and OSResetSystem, which immediately made dozens of anonymous branch targets searchable and semantically useful.
Removed Makefile-level knowledge of sdata/sbss program-header indices from elf2dol; deriving BSS range from program headers is less brittle when object splitting changes section layout.
Aligned DOL header text/data sizes and explicitly zero-padded segment output, a practical tooling fix for section-size drift caused by splitting/matching changes.
Added __start.o to the init object list rather than replacing asm/init.o wholesale, preserving existing init routines while isolating the newly matched startup source.
decomp_lessons: For startup code, a C file can still match when the irreducible parts are written as inline asm and forced into the correct section.

When splitting new objects changes program-header ordering or section boundaries, build tools should calculate layout from ELF metadata instead of relying on hard-coded indices supplied by the Makefile.
Renaming known SDK/runtime functions early pays off across the entire asm tree because branch comments and future decompilation targets become much clearer.
Startup object ordering matters: adding a new .init object may require preserving existing asm/init.o and appending the compiled startup object in the init object list.
Matched startup work can expose padding inaccuracies in extab/extabindex/init arrays/BSS; be ready to remove or resize placeholder padding after a source object starts producing real bytes.
Fixed-address hardware/boot values can be represented in headers with named address constants, but evidence here is limited to this PR's style rather than a reviewed convention.
naming_conventions: Use SDK/runtime names for identified low-level functions: func_800031F4 -> memcpy, func_80003268 -> TRK_memset, func_8034844C -> OSResetSystem.
Use double-underscore names for runtime startup helpers: __start, __check_pad3, __init_registers, __init_data, __init_hardware, __flush_cache.
Use uppercase address macros for fixed boot/runtime addresses, e.g. PAD3_BUTTON_ADDR, EXCEPTIONMASK_ADDR, BOOTINFO2_ADDR, ARENAHI_ADDR, DVD_DEVICECODE_ADDR.
Use struct names matching CodeWarrior/runtime startup metadata: __rom_copy_info and __bss_init_info.
Keep known OS routine names as externs rather than anonymous func_* labels when the SDK identity is clear, e.g. DBInit, OSInit, OSResetSystem, __OSCacheInit, __OSPSInit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declared __start as __declspec(weak) asm void with nofralloc and explicit branches/register setup to reproduce the boot sequence exactly.
Used inline asm labels inside __start for argument parsing and flow control such as _goto_main, _no_args, _end_of_parseargs, _check_pad3, and _goto_end.
Implemented __init_registers in asm to set r1, r2, and r13 from _stack_addr, _SDA2_BASE_, and _SDA_BASE_.
Kept __flush_cache in asm with dcbst/sync/icbi/isync loop behavior, avoiding compiler-generated cache-management differences.
Used small C helpers __copy_rom_section and __init_bss_section around memcpy, memset, and __flush_cache while iterating _rom_copy_info and _bss_init_info sentinel arrays.
Used __declspec(section ".init") extern declarations and #pragma section code_type ".init" to keep startup code and metadata in the intended section.
Adjusted elf2dol to align text/data sizes with DOL_ALIGN in the written header and zero-fill any padding after copying segment bytes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify src/dolphin/os/init/__start.c for improved matching or naming cleanup.
Search for later elf2dol changes around BSS calculation, DOL_ALIGN, fpad, or program-header parsing to see whether this fix was revised.
Check whether Pad3Button fixed-address syntax and __declspec(section ".init") usage became a recurring convention in Dolphin OS startup decompilation.
Look for PRs that further decompile memcpy, TRK_memset, OSResetSystem, DBInit, or OSInit from asm into C.
Investigate build history around object splitting and .init section ordering after __start.o was introduced.
